symptoms.symptom_name: Delayed-onset widespread urticaria after mammalian meat

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Delayed (2-6h) urticaria after red meat is characteristic of alpha-gal-mediated hypersensitivity.
Widespread urticaria is not a feature of enteric infection.
Prominent GI involvement is common in alpha-gal reactions and can dominate the picture.
GI cramps alone are non-specific and overlap with gastroenteritis.
Flushing and sweating reflect systemic mediator release in an allergic reaction; visible on video / patient-reportable.

symptoms.symptom_name: Presyncope / dizziness on standing with tachycardia and relative hypotension

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cardiovascular involvement (presyncope, hypotension, tachycardia) after allergen exposure meets ASCIA anaphylaxis criteria and mandates adrenaline and emergency care.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive escalation of a multi-system reaction with profound fatigue

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An actively worsening multi-system reaction signals ongoing mediator release with unpredictable, potentially biphasic course.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of airway involvement (no throat tightness, stridor or wheeze) at this time

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A currently patent airway does NOT exclude anaphylaxis, which is already met on cardiovascular criteria; must be actively re-screened and safety-netted.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse abdominal tenderness with hyperactive bowel sounds

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diffuse tenderness with hyperactive bowel sounds fits GI smooth-muscle involvement in an allergic reaction; requires in-person examination and cannot be assessed via chat.
Diffuse rather than localised RUQ tenderness argues against biliary pathology.
Elevated specific-IgE to alpha-gal is the confirmatory test; cannot be obtained via telehealth chat and does not change acute emergency management.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Delayed urticaria + GI symptoms + cardiovascular involvement + active escalation together define a systemic allergic reaction meeting anaphylaxis criteria and requiring emergency care.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive confirmation of alpha-gal sensitisation; arranged for follow-up, not the acute encounter.
If drawn in the emergency setting within hours of reaction, supports an anaphylaxis diagnosis; requires in-person/ED phlebotomy.
Diffuse tenderness with systemic allergic features does not fit biliary disease; imaging would distract from emergency allergy management.
